meeting is being recorded.

I could give some context.

So here, the full transparency is that we've been looking to invest in SEO.
And when we say we've been looking to invest in SEO, it's particularly...
Really, for one particular thing, Llama Index is a lot of things, right?
We're known as an agent framework, but we have a lot of different tools and kind of products within our portfolio.
And one of our kind of products is called Llama Cloud, which focuses on a very specific segment that may not be totally apparent to AI builders around document parsing and OCR and stuff like that.
And we were trying to see, if you look at our web traffic today, primarily the majority of it comes from our Llama Index brand, which is a very strong brand.
And then about 10% to 20% comes from non-branded traffic.

So we had a hypothesis that maybe if we kind of up the non-branded traffic, we could increase things like website visits, increase some signups, maybe get some more qualified sales opportunities from folks who might not know what Llama Index is but might have pain points.
And like the adjacent industries that where our solutions look to solve.
That's one hypothesis.
And then where we are kind of in the procurement lifecycle and how we know of GrowthX is I've used GrowthX at Galileo before.

I don't even remember what it was back then.
And then it was a small delivery team.
And we were a pretty early customer.
I saw great value there.
I was largely the one reviewing most of the content and stuff like that that you guys would produce.

So what

That's a product philosophy we have.

And stuff of that sort.
you could help us kind of meet more aggressive targets, right?

working and stuff like that.

I am kind of curious, like what differentiates you guys from, you know, existing SEO agencies?
Yeah, great question.
I would say like the short answer to that question would be our process.
It's very like systematic in terms of there's two phases, eight-week strategy sprint, 12-month growth execution.
In the strategy sprint, we're really focused on generating these artifacts around your audience personas, your company context, your voice and brand guidelines.
And then that allows us to go really fast when it comes to creating high quality content that is going to help you show up in AI search, but being able to do that on a scale, right?
So I'll elaborate on our process so that you can get better understanding of like what we're actually doing during that phase.
But what's nice is, you know, there's that, but there's also the expertise in the loop, you know?
So I think like, I'm not going to go through a bunch of slides for you guys, but on Marcel's side, like he has always believed brands should be publishers.
Every single place he's been, he built an organic content engine and had success doing it.
He was, you know, CMO at scale.
was marketing leader at HashiCorp from six to a hundred million.
And then at Deepgram, he spent thousands of hours building AI workflows to try to replicate like what he had been able to do by hiring big.
Teams, right, at those other places.

And so it's this combination of, like, the expertise that went into the origin of GrowthX combined with, paired with, you know, the process we go through and then the experts who would work with you as a client.

We've got folks who ran SEO for Fanatics and TripAdvisor, and we've also got folks who've done things like buildg2.com website.
Does that answer your question sufficiently?

I think part of this is related to just, like, kind of, we just, you know, if we were to go with you guys, it's because we believe that you guys would move faster than, kind of, like, some of these other agencies that we go with and that we're able to commit to, kind of, reasonably ambitious, like, growth targets, because it is pretty important for the company, and it seems like you guys have executed pretty well.
And I think that is one topic that I'd love to chat about, is to see how we could meet some of the KPIs around, you know, increasing growth.

So I'm just kind of curious what that looks like.
Yeah, absolutely.
And I think like I'd love to just take directly to Marcel what some of those expectations are so that like he can honestly, I mean, right now, just to be honest, like.
it's just the most demand I've ever seen in my, in my career.
I don't, I don't say that like.
Like.
To brag or anything like that, it's more so just like we're kind of in a place where we can be picky about who we partner with just based on mutual fit, right?

Yeah, for sure.

I know you said you have like 70 team members.
Is there like a dedicated owner?
Like how does this work when you're assigned to an account?
Yeah, absolutely.
You get a managing director, a managing editor, and a content manager assigned to your account from our team.
Those team members, they're working on different clients, but it's not a lot of different clients, right?
It's like as many as they can support while delivering consistently.
So you would have three direct dedicated.
And from the KPI perspective, I mean, there's two things we're looking to get.
Like short term, we're looking to just look at increase in non-branded traffic, right?

We have a goal of increasing that by 50% over probably, I mean, I would love for you guys for guidance, but I think a reasonable amount is a six-month period with like a quarterly tracker of like to see how we're able to kind of move that non-branded traffic up.
And then eventually what we need help with as well as instrumentation and to make sure that what content engines lead to signups and what content engine leads to MQLs and stuff like that.

So we'd like to see that, but immediately I wouldn't want to index on those two things because I understand that the traffic comes before kind of the measurement of the downstream signups and the MQLs, but just have a system in place so that that is something we could, check long-term.
Makes sense.
That's perfectly clear.

Okay.

As we define some content clusters and stuff like that.

And then when you hit that stride, then you execute faster after the eight weeks and then you're in phase two, right?
That's what the phase one, phase two split is meant to show.
We want to align with you on what great looks like before we, you know, put the foot fully on the gas.
And we all.

Exactly.
And you would...

It's just slower and not to the extent that it's in phase two.
Exactly.
And we give you the ability to opt out by the seventh week of the eight-week engagement.
So, you know, the idea is that before we move into growth execution, which is daily publishing for all of our clients, you understand what the caliber of the content we'd be creating looks like.
